acceptance_targets: [chapter_12_prose_01] Pass if the exact construction "did not answer" appears no more than once across the revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:163, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:283, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:371, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:519, and each silence reads as a different refusal when those passages are read in context. Fail if multiple key beats still announce silence with the same stock clause.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the exact construction "did not answer" appears no more than once across the revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:163, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:283, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:371, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:519, and each silence reads as a different refusal when those passages are read in context. Fail if multiple key beats still announce silence with the same stock clause.
findings.evidence: The chapter marks silence with the same clause three times: "She did not answer." (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:163), "Lena did not answer." (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:371), and "Lena did not answer." (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:519). A related variant, "June did not answer quickly enough," also appears at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:283.
findings.problem: In a chapter built around withheld speech, repeating the same sentence shape turns refusal into visible scaffolding. These are different emotional beats, but the prose keeps announcing them with the same stock construction, which flattens the chapter's silence rather than making each silence newly legible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least two of the silence markers at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:163, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:283, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:371, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:519 with distinct physical or social consequences tied to the moment: stalled movement, redirected gaze, a swallowed reply, a task performed too hard, or another refusal that costs the other person something on the page.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
